verdict_reasoning: B2M (beta-2-microglobulin) is a soluble protein that serves as a stable non-covalent light-chain partner of the MHC class I heavy chain. While B2M itself lacks a transmembrane domain, it is assembled intracellularly with the MHC class I heavy chain (which is membrane-anchored) and co-trafficked to the cell surface as an integral component of the MHC class I complex. The NCBI summary explicitly confirms B2M is found 'in association with the major histocompatibility complex (MHC) class I heavy chain on the surface of nearly all nucleated cells.' B2M remains stably bound to the surface-resident MHC class I complex and is not in equilibrium with a soluble pool during surface expression.
